headline: Finalized Lido claims stay open; governance can still freeze new exits
rationale: Steel-man red: a governance-linked reseal path can keep withdrawal requests and finalization paused indefinitely. Steel-man orange: claimWithdrawals and claimWithdrawal stay callable while request placement and finalization can be halted, with a 14-day GateSeal committee emergency path and a separate governance-linked reseal path. Steel-man green: finalized exits are permissionless on-chain. Choosing orange because finalized claims are pause-exempt, but new exits and finalization can still be frozen beyond 14 days through governance-linked reseal.

evidence.shows: Deployed Ethereum mainnet WithdrawalQueue proxy; explorer page links the implementation and shows recent direct user transactions invoking Request Withdrawal and Claim Withdrawal methods, supporting that exit functions are callable on-chain without the frontend.
Maps the mainnet WithdrawalQueueERC721 proxy at 0x889edC2eDab5f40e902b864aD4d7AdE8E412F9B1 to implementation 0xE42C659Dc09109566720EA8b2De186c2Be7D94D9 and identifies the implementation source file.

Enumerates exit functions: requestWithdrawals, requestWithdrawalsWstETH, requestWithdrawalsWithPermit, requestWithdrawalsWstETHWithPermit, claimWithdrawalsTo, claimWithdrawals, claimWithdrawal. request paths call _checkResumed(); pauseFor and pauseUntil are onlyRole(PAUSE_ROLE) and explicitly state finalized claims remain available; claim paths are ownership and finalization checks and are not wrapped in _checkResumed().

Lists WithdrawalQueue role holders: PAUSE_ROLE is held by the Withdrawal Queue GateSeal and the Reseal Manager; RESUME_ROLE is held by the Reseal Manager. Also states protocol proxy admins are set to the Lido DAO Agent, tying the slow path to DAO governance rather than a unilateral operator.

Describes GateSeal as a committee-operated one-time immediate emergency pause with pause duration capped at 14 days; identifies the active WithdrawalQueue GateSeal address; and states the ResealManager can extend a temporary pause into a governance-controlled pause until governance explicitly unpauses.

Verified Ethereum contract for the ResealManager. The ABI exposes reseal, resume, PAUSE_INFINITELY, EMERGENCY_PROTECTED_TIMELOCK, and CallerIsNotGovernance, supporting that the indefinite pause extension path is implemented via a governance-linked contract rather than the emergency committee directly.
Verified Ethereum contract for the WithdrawalQueue GateSeal, the emergency pause component referenced in Lido docs for the queue.

unknowns: E5: I could not determine a protocol-enforced maximum queue duration or a protocol-enforced daily withdrawal cap from the allowed sources checked.

E6: No dedicated forced-exit or escape-hatch function beyond the standard request and claim path was identified in the checked sources, but I could not prove its nonexistence exhaustively from the allowed sources alone.
